objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 health/body words, 'have got' grammar for possession and illness, listening, speaking, homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. 20 эрүүл мэндийн үг, 'have got' дүрэм, сонсох, ярих, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Talk about body parts and illness using 'I've got / She's got + a/an + symptom'.
contentMn: Биеийн эрхтэн ба өвчний тухай 'I've got / She's got + a/an + шинж тэмдэг' хэлбэрээр ярина.

vocabulary.example: I can hear with my ears.
Brush your teeth twice a day!
My arm is very sore today.
I hurt my leg playing football.
I've got a cold this week.
He has got a bad cough.
Take this medicine three times a day.
I need to see a doctor today.

quiz.question: Why wasn't Bat at school?
quiz.explanation: Bat told Sarnai he has a cold.

quiz.question: What is Bat's temperature?
quiz.explanation: He said his temperature is 38 degrees.

quiz.question: Who gave Bat medicine?
quiz.explanation: His mother gave him medicine and hot soup.

quiz.question: What does Sarnai advise?
options: see a doctor if not better
quiz.answer: see a doctor if not better
quiz.explanation: Sarnai says to see a doctor if he isn't better tomorrow.

quiz.question: What tense is 'have got' most similar to?
Has Bat got a fever?
quiz.explanation: He said he has a small fever (38°C).

page1.grammarTopic: Have got — I've got / She's got — possession and illness
page1.grammarExplanation: 'Have got' means the same as 'have' — very common in spoken British English. For I/You/We/They use 'have got'; for He/She/It use 'has got'. Short forms: I've got, she's got. Used for possession ('I've got a book.') and illness ('I've got a headache.'). 'Have got' нь 'have'-тай ижил утгатай. I/You/We/They — 'have got'; He/She/It — 'has got'. Эзэмшил болон өвчний тухай хэлэхэд хэрэглэнэ.
commonMistakes: She have got a cold. → She HAS GOT a cold.
I've got headache. → I've got A headache.
Has she got fever? → Has she got A fever?
I hasn't got time. → I HAVEN'T got time.

page2.readingPassage: Bat is not at school today. He has got a bad cold. He has also got a headache and a fever. His mother says: 'You have got to rest today!' She gives him hot tea and medicine. His friend Sarnai calls him: 'Have you got a cough too?' 'Yes, I've got a cough,' says Bat. 'You should see a doctor!' says Sarnai. 'I will go tomorrow if I haven't got better,' says Bat.
speakingQuestions: 1. How are you feeling today? / Та өнөөдөр яаж мэдэрч байна?
2. Have you got a cold this week?
3. What do you do when you have a headache?
4. Have you got any medicine at home?
5. When did you last see a doctor?
6. What is good for a cold? / Ханиадны хувьд юу сайн вэ?

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: You don't look well. What's wrong?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I've got a terrible headache and a fever.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Oh no! Have you got any medicine?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes, my mother gave me some. But I feel very bad.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: You should see a doctor today.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I know. My mother is taking me this afternoon.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Have you got a cough too?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes, I've got a cough and a sore throat.

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Sarnai: Bat, why weren't you at school today? Bat: I've got a cold. I've also got a headache and a sore throat. Sarnai: That sounds terrible! Have you got a fever too? Bat: Yes, I've got a small fever. My temperature is 38 degrees. Sarnai: Have you got any medicine at home? Bat: Yes. My mother gave me medicine and hot soup. Sarnai: You should see a doctor if you haven't got better by tomorrow. Bat: I will. Thank you for calling, Sarnai!

listeningQuestions.question: What has Bat got? (all of them)
Has Bat got medicine at home?
What did his mother give him?
What does Sarnai advise?
options: see a doctor if not better
listeningQuestions.answer: see a doctor if not better

listeningQuestions.question: How does Bat feel about Sarnai calling?
What tense is 'have got' most similar to?

grammarPractice: 5. I _____ a cold this week. (have got)
homework: Writing: Write 5 sentences about your health using 'I've got' or 'I haven't got'.
Vocabulary: Write all body parts from today's lesson 3 times each.
Speaking: Ask a family member: 'Have you got any medicine at home?'
Review: Week 2 complete! Review all 140 words from Days 8-14 before sleeping.
answerKey: Fill in blanks: 1-have got · 2-has got · 3-Have · 4-hasn't got · 5-have got · 6-Has
completionSummary: Listened to a phone call dialog
